paper_focus.title: Chemical Composition Optimization for Austenitic Steels of the Fe-Mn-Al-C System
paper_focus.research_objective: To determine the best combination of composition ranges for basic alloying elements in Fe-Mn-Al-C steels to obtain an optimum microstructural state (absence of δ-ferrite, coarse grain-boundary precipitation, and β-Mn) and a combination of tensile strength ≥ 1100 MPa and impact toughness ≥ 50 J/cm² for structural applications.
main_process_variables: Alloy composition: Al (3–10 wt%), C (0.3–1.2 wt%), Mn (24–34 wt%)
Mechanical testing: tensile strength (UTS), U-notch impact toughness (K_CU)
